author: lOrm
content:
Resolved. New module `src/plugins/validate.rs` + wired into every git-source entry point.

**`validate_source_url(raw)` — enforces:**
- Scheme ∈ `ALLOWED_URL_SCHEMES = ["https", "ssh"]`. Rejects `http`, `file`, `git`, `ftp`, `ldap`, etc.
- SCP-style shorthand `git@host:owner/repo.git` accepted (recognized via `looks_like_scp_ssh`, since `url::Url` refuses it).
- Host must be present and non-empty.
- **Scheme-aware credential rule:** a bare username (`ssh://git@host/...`) is allowed ONLY for `ssh://`. An inline password is rejected for every scheme including ssh. https refuses both. Credentials belong in git credential helpers, never in a marketplace manifest that may be logged or committed.

**Enforcement site:** `git_clone(url, dest, git_ref)` in `src/plugins/git.rs` calls `validate_source_url` before invoking the `git` binary. Every path that reaches `git clone` is guaranteed to have gone through the check, regardless of caller route. This replaces the 'trust the caller' posture the codebase had.

**Additional validation at public callers:**
- `add_marketplace_from_git(url, ...)` validates up front (saves the `mkdir` when URL will be rejected).
- `install_from_git(url, ...)` validates up front.
- `git_clone` itself re-validates — intentionally redundant, cheap defense-in-depth.

**Headers sanitization (mandated point 3):** not yet wired — `MarketplaceSource::Url` has no active fetch implementation today; flagged as a follow-up for when that source type gets implemented. The type exists but has no dispatch site I could find in the current tree.

**Tests:** 15 unit tests in `plugins::validate::tests` covering https/ssh/SCP accept paths, http/file/git/ftp/gibberish reject paths, userinfo handling for both https and ssh, derive-name from https URLs, SCP URLs, traversal segments, trailing-dot segments, and empty-path URLs. All 610 lib + 130 integration tests pass.

**Out of scope / follow-up:**
- `MarketplaceSource::Url` header allowlist (User-Agent / Authorization / Accept only) + CRLF sanitization via `http::HeaderValue::from_str` — needs a fetch implementation to plug into.
- TLS pinning — separate, larger concern.
- Plugin manifest signature verification (tracked by #249).
